text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. It is similar to cleaning and organizing a room: everything functions the same way, but it becomes much easier to find and use things. The main goal is to increase readability, making the code simpler to understand and maintain. Over time, software projects often accumulate technical debt, which is the extra work created by using quick, messy solutions. Refactoring helps pay off this debt by making the code cleaner and more efficient. Regular refactoring saves time in the long run, as developers can more easily add new features and fix bugs.
